steps.title: Find the causal link
steps.content: The passage explains that birds sing at lower frequencies in dense vegetation BECAUSE of what dense vegetation does to high-frequency sounds. For this explanation to work, dense vegetation must disadvantage high-frequency sounds—specifically, it must reduce the distance they can travel.

steps.title: Match the reducing effect
steps.content: ”Diminish” means to reduce or make smaller, which precisely captures how dense vegetation limits the distance high-frequency sounds travel. The other choices don't work: “exceed” suggests sounds go farther (backward), “encompass” and “conceal” don't address distance at all. That matches choice B — B is correct.
